data.intro: ECMAScript (/ˈɛk.smə.skrɪpt/ ˈɛk.smə.skrɪpt/), also known as ECMAScript, is the standard for the JavaScript programming language, intended to ensure the interoperability of web pages across different web browsers.[2][3] It is standardized by Ecma International's TC39 technical committee in the document ECMA-262, with i18n ("Intl") APIs standardized in the document ECMA-402.[3]
